content.problemStatement:
JJ has an array AAA initially of length NNN. He can perform the following operation on AAA:

**1)** Pick any index iii (1‚â§i‚â§‚à£A‚à£)(1 \le i \le |A|)(1‚â§i‚â§‚à£A‚à£) such that Ai>1A_i \gt 1Ai‚Äã>1 \\
**2)** Select any two integers XXX and YYY such that X+Y=AiX + Y = A_iX+Y=Ai‚Äã and X,Y‚â•1X, Y \ge 1X,Y‚â•1 \\
**3)** Replace AiA_iAi‚Äã with XXX and YYY

Note that the length of the array increases by 111 after each operation.

For example, if A=[4,6,7,2]A = [4, 6, 7, 2]A=[4,6,7,2], he can select i=1i = 1i=1 and select X=1,Y=3X = 1, Y = 3X=1,Y=3 (since X+Y=A1X + Y = A_1X+Y=A1‚Äã). After the operation array becomes: [4‚Äæ,6,7,2]‚Üí[1‚Äæ,3‚Äæ,6,7,2][\underline{4}, 6, 7, 2] \rightarrow [\underline{1}, \underline{3}, 6, 7, 2][4‚Äã,6,7,2]‚Üí[1‚Äã,3‚Äã,6,7,2].

JJ wants to make AAA palindromic. Find the minimum number of operations to do so.

It is guaranteed that AAA can be converted to a palindromic array by using the above operation.

Note: An array is called a palindrome if it reads the same backwards and forwards, for e.g. [1,3,3,1][1, 3, 3, 1][1,3,3,1] and [6,2,6][6, 2, 6][6,2,6] are palindromic.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚Äî the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations to make AAA palindromic.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

[3,1,6,6,4‚Äæ]‚Üíi=5,X=1,Y=3[3,1,6,6,1‚Äæ,3‚Äæ][3, 1, 6, 6, \underline{4}] \xrightarrow{i = 5, X = 1, Y = 3} [3, 1, 6, 6, \underline{1}, \underline{3}][3,1,6,6,4‚Äã]i=5,X=1,Y=3‚Äã[3,1,6,6,1‚Äã,3‚Äã]

Test Case 2: AAA is already palindromic.
